ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class; no specific node for flavoured white coffee with non-dairy creamer and no added sugar. 'closest_parent' used as it's the most specific available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJL covers ready-to-drink coffee broadly; no specific facet for flavoured white coffee without sugar. 'closest_parent' used as it's the most specific available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 ml (one can or bottle), equivalent to 240 g, providing roughly 120 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured white coffee (no sugar) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Flavoured white coffee (no sugar)', cr_id D09010031, with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for flavoured white coffee (no sugar) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 41 (low) based on the closest analogous entry: instant coffee with non-dairy creamer and low sugar content, which yields a low GI due to the dominant fat and protein contribution from the creamer slowing glucose absorption. Carbohydrate per serving = 7.14 g/100g × 240 g serving = 17.1 g; GL = (41 × 17.1) / 100 = 7.0, rounded to 7.1. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary by formulation and sweetener type.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 46 mg/100 g; palm oil-based creamer contributes saturated fat; sweetened beverage pattern even at low sugar levels
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this beverage to occasional use; the sodium content (46 mg/100 g, ~110 mg per 240 ml serving) is moderate, but the palm oil-based creamer adds saturated fat that adversely affects vascular tone, and the sweetened instant-drink pattern is associated with poorer blood pressure outcomes — plain black coffee or unsweetened tea is preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 1.72 g/100 g in a sweetened beverage context; palm oil-based creamer worsens insulin resistance; low GI (estimated 41) partially mitigates acute glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should treat this as a sweetened beverage despite the 'no sugar' label — the 1.72 g sugar/100 g (approximately 4.1 g per 240 ml serving) combined with palm oil-based creamer that impairs insulin sensitivity makes regular consumption inadvisable; if consumed, limit to one serving and account for the ~17 g carbohydrate load per serving in the daily meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 1.4 g/100 g from palm oil-based non-dairy creamer; low potassium; ultra-processed beverage category
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this beverage is a moderate concern due to the palm oil-derived saturated fat (~3.4 g per 240 ml serving) which raises LDL cholesterol, the ultra-processed formulation, and the absence of cardioprotective nutrients such as potassium, omega-3 fatty acids, or dietary fibre above 1 g/100 g; substitution with plain black coffee or a dairy-milk coffee is recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm oil-based saturated fat is pro-inflammatory; no qualifying anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this beverage as the palm oil-based non-dairy creamer contributes pro-inflammatory saturated fat (~1.4 g/100 g) without any offsetting anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or whole-food fibre above 3 g/100 g; plain black coffee provides chlorogenic acid polyphenols without the inflammatory creamer component.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured White Coffee (No Sugar) is a ready-to-drink (RTD) commercial coffee beverage popular in Singapore and Malaysia, formulated with coffee extract, palm oil-based non-dairy creamer, food flavourings, and typically a non-caloric sweetener in place of table sugar. The 'no sugar' designation indicates no added sucrose or table sugar, but the product still contains 1.72 g of sugar per 100 g (likely from the creamer or trace sources) and 7.14 g of total carbohydrate per 100 g. Per 100 g, it provides 50 kcal, 0.49 g protein, 1.97 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm oil, approximately 1.4 g/100 g), and 0.98 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 46 mg/100 g, making it relatively low in sodium. A standard 240 ml serving delivers approximately 120 kcal, 17 g carbohydrate, and 3.4 g saturated fat. The beverage has a creamy, mildly sweet coffee flavour with a smooth texture due to the non-dairy creamer. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning pick-me-up in Singapore, Malaysia, and across Southeast Asia. Clinically, despite the low-sugar label, this product is classified as a sweetened beverage for dietary counselling purposes due to residual sugar content and the pro-inflammatory and insulin-resistance-worsening effects of palm oil-based creamer. The estimated glycaemic index is low (approximately 41), but the glycaemic load per serving is approximately 7, which is non-trivial for individuals managing type 2 diabetes. It is ultra-processed and not recommended for regular consumption by individuals with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010031) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate (low confidence for GI specifically) but all macros and secondary nutrients are exact from HPB. Overall record confidence is rated high due to exact HPB match for nutritional data.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 1.40 g/100 g based on the known composition of palm oil-based non-dairy creamer contributing the majority of the 1.97 g total fat; this is an internal estimate as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry — monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, all vitamins, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid, mineral, and moisture data for this RTD flavoured white coffee are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 2. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database entry D09010031). 3. GI of 41 is an analogous estimate based on instant coffee with non-dairy creamer from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. 4. The 'no sugar' label on this product refers to no added table sugar (sucrose); the product still contains 1.72 g sugar/100 g, likely from the non-dairy creamer or trace sources, and should be treated as a sweetened beverage for clinical dietary counselling. 5. Halal status assumed based on typical commercial formulation in Singapore/Malaysia; verify product label for certification. 6. Vegan status is uncertain due to possible milk derivatives in non-dairy creamer; is_vegan set to false pending label verification.
